Barack Obama:
Biography of Barack Obama
Birth and Childhood:
Barack Hussein Obama was born on 4 August, 1961 in the colony Karnak place of Kenya and lived in a local school of the state of Hawaii. His father Barack Obama worked in a small local school of Hawaii, while his mother Marty Obama came from Kenya. Their relationship did not last, but their child Barack grew up in Hawaii. He grew up in Hawaii and studied in school and college.
Education and Career:
Obama obtained a bachelor degree in computer science from the University of Hawaii. Later he received a master’s degree in political science from Columbia University. He worked as a community organizer with local students who came from Kenya and at that time he became acquainted with a student named Michelle Robinson. They were married in 1989, and they have two daughters—Marli and Marli Obama.
Political Career:
In 1996 he became a member of the local parliament of the state of Illinois and later became a member of parliament of that state. During his parliamentary work he worked especially on health and social culture. In 2004 he became a member of parliament of the state of Illinois and during his first parliamentary competition he became especially popular on issues of social relations and health.
Work as President:
In 2008 he became the first African-American President of America. During his first competition he became especially popular on issues of social relations and health. Shimon Peres Biography
 Birth and Childhood:
 Shimon Peres (born in 1923) was a founding leader and president of Israel. He was born in a small town in Poland, Berlin, but his family was established in Israel. His childhood and youth were closely connected with Israel’s independence war.
Early Life and Political Work:
 Peres played an important role in Israel’s independence war. He was a member of Haganah (Israel’s independence organization) and during that time he had a special relationship with Israel’s first prime minister David Ben-Gurion. He had a slight relationship with Israel’s first prime minister Ben-Gurion and had a slight relationship with him.
Political Work and Establishment as Prime Minister:
 Peres worked in various positions related to Israel’s power. In 1954 he had a slight relationship with Israel’s first prime minister David Ben-Gurion and had a slight relationship with him. In 1955 he had a slight relationship with Israel’s first prime minister David Ben-Gurion and had a slight relationship with him.
Establishment as President:
 Peres served as President of Israel from 2007 to 2014. He worked as the first president of Israel and his main agenda was to implement the peace process between Israel and Palestine. He was the main representative of the Oslo Agreement (1993), where relations were established between representatives of Israel and Palestine.

Dilma Rousseff:
Biography of Dilma Rousseff

Early Life and Education
Dilma Rousseff (Dilma Rousseff) was born on 12 November, 1947 in the city of Rio de Janeiro of Brazil. She was born in a low-income family, whose father was a functional employee and mother was a girls’ teacher. She graduated in engineering from a famous engineering university of Brazil and later obtained a master’s degree in management and planning from an established university of Brazil.

Early Career and Political Activities
Dilma Rousseff began her career by working in the public sector of Brazil. She worked in the petroleum company Petrobras of Brazil and later became a government official of Brazil. She entered political life by joining the Workers’ Party (PT) of Brazil. She became a federal deputy in the 1980s and later, became a senator. In 2006 she became the president of the Chamber of Deputies of Brazil, which was an important position of her political activities.

Work as President
In 2010 Dilma Rousseff became the president of Brazil, through which she became the first female president in the history of Brazil. During her time Brazil was a period of economic development, where development of the commodity market and social projects played an important role in the distribution of wealth of the society. She had launched huge projects in the distribution of social wealth of Brazil and in the fields of health and education.
